title: Cairo ⇄ Luxor
summary: The full sweep of the Nile aboard a traditional dahabiya under tow — from the Pyramids of Giza through Middle Egypt's hidden monuments to the temple cities of Thebes.
description: A rare twelve-night voyage along the river's least-travelled reaches, linking Cairo with Luxor. Traditional dahabiya cruising, gently towed by a support tugboat for the long passage, with daily international buffet dining and the signature sunset tea on deck. Sailing times are longer than conventional cruisers — that is the point: this is the slow, authentic Nile.
routeOverview: Twelve nights spanning Middle and Upper Egypt — the pyramids of Giza and Fayoum, the painted tombs of Tell el-Amarna and Beni Hassan, the sacred temples of Abydos and Dendera, and the grand monuments of Luxor. Sailable in either direction; northbound (Luxor → Cairo) runs with the current and is noticeably faster.

timeline.title: Arrival in Cairo & Embarkation
timeline.description: Arrive in Cairo and transfer to your dahabiya for embarkation, with a welcome dinner on board. Overnight in Cairo.

timeline.title: Cairo Sightseeing
timeline.description: Visit the legendary Pyramids of Giza and the renowned Egyptian Museum. All meals on board, including sunset tea with snacks. Overnight in Cairo.

timeline.title: Sail from Cairo to Beni Suef
timeline.description: Begin your Nile journey sailing south through the fertile landscapes of Middle Egypt. Sailing: approx. 120 km · 16–18 hrs against the current. Overnight in Beni Suef.

timeline.title: Fayoum Excursion
timeline.description: Excursion to the ancient pyramids of Hawara and Meidum. Sunset tea and dinner on board. Overnight in Beni Suef.

timeline.title: Sail to Minya — Tuna el-Gebel & Ashmunein
timeline.description: Continue south to Minya, with visits to Tuna el-Gebel and Ashmunein. Sailing: approx. 145 km · 19–21 hrs against the current. Overnight in Minya.

timeline.title: Tell el-Amarna & Beni Hassan
timeline.description: Explore Tell el-Amarna — the Northern Tombs and the Tomb of Ahmose (EA3) — followed by the impressive rock-cut tombs of Beni Hassan. Overnight in Minya.

timeline.title: Sail to Asyut
timeline.description: A full day sailing south toward Asyut, watching Middle Egypt drift by from the sundeck. Sailing: approx. 125 km · 17–18 hrs against the current. Overnight in Asyut.

timeline.title: Sail to Sohag & Abydos
timeline.description: Continue south to Sohag with an excursion to the magnificent Temple of Abydos, dedicated to Osiris. Sailing: approx. 100 km · 14–15 hrs against the current. Overnight in Sohag.

timeline.title: Sail to Qena
timeline.description: A leisurely sailing day toward Qena, enjoying panoramic Nile scenery from the sundeck. Sailing: approx. 160 km · 21–23 hrs against the current. Overnight in Qena.

timeline.title: Dendera Temple & Sail to Luxor
timeline.description: Visit the beautifully preserved Temple of Dendara, dedicated to Hathor, before continuing to Luxor. Sailing: approx. 65 km · 9–10 hrs against the current. Overnight in Luxor.

timeline.title: Valley of the Kings & Hatshepsut
timeline.description: Discover the Valley of the Kings and the spectacular mortuary Temple of Hatshepsut at Deir el-Bahari. Overnight in Luxor.

timeline.title: Karnak & Luxor Temples — Disembarkation
timeline.description: Visit the monumental Karnak Temple complex and the elegant Luxor Temple before disembarkation, with a farewell dinner on board. Overnight in Luxor.

timeline.title: Departure
timeline.description: Transfer to the airport for your onward international flight.

timeline.title: Arrival in Luxor & Embarkation
timeline.description: Arrive in Luxor and transfer to your dahabiya for embarkation, with a welcome dinner on board. Overnight in Luxor.

timeline.title: Valley of the Kings & Hatshepsut
timeline.description: Discover the West Bank — the Valley of the Kings and the spectacular mortuary Temple of Hatshepsut. All meals on board, including sunset tea. Overnight in Luxor.

timeline.title: Karnak & Luxor Temples — Sail to Qena
timeline.description: Visit the monumental Karnak Temple complex and Luxor Temple before beginning your northbound journey. Sailing: approx. 65 km · 7–8 hrs with the current. Overnight in Qena.

timeline.title: Dendera Temple — Sail toward Sohag
timeline.description: Visit the beautifully preserved Temple of Dendara before continuing north. Sailing: approx. 160 km · 17–19 hrs with the current. Overnight in Sohag.

timeline.title: Abydos — Sail to Asyut
timeline.description: Excursion to the magnificent Temple of Abydos, then continued sailing toward Asyut. Sailing: approx. 100 km · 11–12 hrs with the current. Overnight in Asyut.

timeline.title: Sail to Minya
timeline.description: A full, leisurely day sailing north through Middle Egypt, panoramic scenery from the sundeck. Sailing: approx. 125 km · 14–15 hrs with the current. Overnight in Minya.

timeline.title: Tell el-Amarna & Beni Hassan
timeline.description: Explore Tell el-Amarna — the Northern Tombs and the Tomb of Ahmose (EA3) — followed by the tombs of Beni Hassan. Overnight in Minya.

timeline.title: Tuna el-Gebel & Ashmunein — Sail to Beni Suef
timeline.description: Visit Tuna el-Gebel and Ashmunein before continuing north. Sailing: approx. 145 km · 16–17 hrs with the current. Overnight in Beni Suef.

timeline.title: Fayoum Excursion
timeline.description: Excursion to the ancient pyramids of Hawara and Meidum. Sunset tea and dinner on board. Overnight in Beni Suef.

timeline.title: Sail from Beni Suef to Cairo
timeline.description: Continue your scenic voyage north toward Cairo through the fertile landscapes of Middle Egypt. Sailing: approx. 120 km · 13–15 hrs with the current. Overnight in Cairo.

timeline.title: Cairo Sightseeing
timeline.description: Visit the legendary Pyramids of Giza and the renowned Egyptian Museum. All meals on board, including sunset tea. Overnight in Cairo.

timeline.title: Old Cairo & Disembarkation Preparations
timeline.description: Optional exploration of Old Cairo and the Khan el-Khalili bazaar, or leisure on board. Farewell dinner. Overnight in Cairo.

timeline.title: Departure
timeline.description: Transfer to the airport for your onward international flight.

faq.q: Why are the sailing times so long?
faq.a: This is a traditional dahabiya towed by a support tugboat for the long Cairo–Luxor passage. Sailing times are far longer than conventional Nile cruisers — by design. The reward is the slow, authentic river and access to Middle Egypt's rarely-visited monuments.
Northbound (Luxor → Cairo) sails with the Nile current and is roughly 15–20% faster than the southbound Cairo → Luxor route. Both visit the same sites; we tailor the direction to your travel plans.

Very few vessels sail the full Cairo–Luxor reach. You see Giza and the Fayoum pyramids, the painted tombs of Tell el-Amarna and Beni Hassan, and the sacred temples of Abydos and Dendera — alongside the great monuments of Thebes.
